description_long: This is a large, cylindrical wooden barrel with a weathered, grayish-brown appearance, featuring visible wood grain and signs of age. The barrel is reinforced with three wide wooden hoops encircling its body and a vertical wooden strip running down one side for added structural support. The top surface is flat and appears to be made of the same wood as the sides, with a small, round metal bung or plug set off-center, likely for filling or emptying contents. The wood shows darkened areas and stains, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements or use in storage. There are no visible handles or decorative markings, giving it a utilitarian, rustic look. This type of barrel is commonly found in outdoor or semi-industrial urban settings, such as alleys, storage yards, or near garages.
description: A large, weathered wooden barrel with wide wooden hoops and a flat top featuring a small metal bung. The surface is rustic and stained, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows the flat wooden top with a visible metal bung and three horizontal wooden hoops, with the wood grain and stains clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the offset placement of the metal bung and the curvature of the barrel, with the hoops and wood texture prominent.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the continuous wooden hoops and the uniform, weathered wood surface, with minimal visible hardware.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a vertical wooden reinforcement strip running down the side, in addition to the hoops and the metal bung on the top.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: wooden hoops and vertical wooden strip
